mainText:
Good morning. Here's your Breeze communications briefing for Monday, April 6th.

No new Breeze coverage over the weekend. The airline enters Easter week with a clean news cycle while the broader industry weathered one of the most operationally turbulent holiday periods in recent memory.

Now for industry and competitor news.

Bloomberg reported Saturday that jet fuel has more than doubled to nearly 197 dollars per barrel as the Iran war's closure of the Strait of Hormuz chokes global oil supply, with global flight cancellation rates approaching 7 percent last week and U.S. domestic fares jumping sharply as carriers race to cover surging costs. Breeze's shorter-haul domestic routes carry significantly lower fuel exposure than long-haul carriers, but leadership should begin modeling fuel surcharge scenarios now before rising costs force reactive rather than proactive decisions.

Greek City Times reported Friday that Qatar Airways has suspended service to 64 destinations as the Iran conflict grounds Middle East-based carriers and disrupts regional airspace, while western airlines including Lufthansa, British Airways, and Air France are moving quickly to redeploy aircraft to North American routes to fill the void. The collapse in Middle East hub connectivity could redirect some international leisure travelers toward domestic alternatives — worth flagging to marketing to monitor whether any Breeze markets see booking upticks in the coming weeks.

The Traveler reported Easter Sunday that Florida's three major hubs — Orlando, Miami, and Fort Lauderdale — saw patchy disruptions as the Easter weekend's national total climbed past 5,600 delays, driven by spring storms, peak holiday volumes, and T.S.A. staffing gaps. Breeze's point-to-point model through secondary airports kept the carrier largely clear of the hub-cascade disruptions all weekend — communications should make that operational advantage visible on social media and in outreach to local market reporters this week.

That's your briefing. Roger is standing by when you're ready to dive deeper or take action on any of these stories.
